entries.content: To flush the DNS cache in Windows, open PowerShell as Administrator and run: `Clear-DnsClientCache`. Alternatively, in CMD use `ipconfig /flushdns`.
To restart a network adapter via PowerShell, use: `Restart-NetAdapter -Name 'Wi-Fi' -Confirm:$false`.
If PowerShell scripts are blocked from running, set the execution policy to RemoteSigned for the current user: `Set-ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned -Scope CurrentUser -Force`.
To reset the Windows Firewall to its default settings, run: `netsh advfirewall reset` in an elevated command prompt.
